metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter III - GENERAL OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITIES

summary: Section 28(10) requires registered credit rating agencies to establish professional rating committees composed of qualified members, with all rating decisions reserved exclusively for these committees.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (5) An investment adviser shall maintain an arm’s-length relationship between its activities as an investment adviser and its other activities
summary: Section 34(5) requires an investment adviser to maintain an arm's-length relationship between its investment advisory activities and any other business activities.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (6) An investment adviser shall not enter into transactions on its own account which is contrary to the advice given to its clients for a period of 15 days from the day of such advice: Provided that during the period of 15 days, if the investment adviser is of the opinion that the situation has
summary: Section 34(6) restricts an investment adviser from entering into personal transactions contrary to client advice for a 15-day period following the provision of such advice.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: adviser is of the opinion that the situation has changed, then it may enter into such a transaction on its own account after giving such revised assessment to the client at least 24 hours in advance of entering into such transaction
summary: Under the proviso to Section 34(6), an investment adviser may trade on its own account within the 15-day restricted period if the situation changes, provided a revised assessment is communicated to the client at least 24 hours in advance.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (7) An investment adviser shall not act on its own account, knowingly to sell securities or investment products to or purchase securities or investment products from a client
summary: Section 34(7) prohibits an investment adviser from knowingly selling securities to or purchasing securities from a client on its own account.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (8) An investment adviser shall ensure that, for the purposes of risk profiling, - (a) it obtains such information from the client as is necessary for the purpose of giving investment advice; (b) it has a process for assessing the risk a client is willing and able to take; (c) risk profile of the
summary: Section 34(8)(a)-(b) requires an investment adviser to collect necessary client information and establish a formal process to assess the client's risk appetite and capacity.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: willing and able to take; (c) risk profile of the client is communicated to the client after completion of risk assessment; and (d) the information provided by clients and their risk assessment is updated periodically
summary: Section 34(8)(c)-(d) mandates that the investment adviser communicate the assessed risk profile to the client and ensure periodic updates of client information and risk assessments.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (9) An investment adviser shall ensure that all investments on which investment advice is provided is suitable to the risk profile of the client and is consistent with the client’s investment objectives and financial position
summary: Section 34(9) requires an investment adviser to ensure that all advice provided is suitable to the client's risk profile, investment objectives, and financial position.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (10) An investment adviser shall have client level segregation for investment advisory and distribution services
summary: Section 34(10) mandates that an investment adviser must maintain client-level segregation between investment advisory services and distribution services.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (11) An investment adviser shall maintain an arm’s length relationship between its activities as investment adviser and distributor by providing advisory services through a separately identifiable department or division, including separate manpower for conduct of these activities
summary: Section 34(11) mandates that investment advisers maintain an arm's length relationship between advisory and distribution activities by utilizing a separately identifiable department or division with dedicated manpower.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (12) An investment adviser may provide implementation services to its advisory clients in securities market: Provided that the investment adviser shall ensure the following: (a) the potential conflicts of interest, if any, are adequately disclosed to its clients; and (b) the fee charged, if any,
summary: Section 34(12) permits investment advisers to provide implementation services to advisory clients, provided that all potential conflicts of interest are adequately disclosed.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: to its clients; and (b) the fee charged, if any, for the implementation services is pursuant to an agreement with its clients and disclosed in a transparent manner
summary: Section 34(12)(b) requires that any fees charged for implementation services must be governed by a client agreement and disclosed in a transparent manner.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (13) The client shall not be under any obligation to avail implementation services offered by the investment adviser.
summary: Section 34(13) establishes that clients are under no obligation to utilize implementation services offered by their investment adviser.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (1) An investment banker shall enter into an agreement with the issuer of securities specifying the roles and responsibilities of the investment banker in the issue
summary: Section 35(1) requires investment bankers to execute a formal agreement with the securities issuer that explicitly defines their roles and responsibilities.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (2) Where there is more than one lead investment banker to the issue, the responsibilities of each of such lead investment banker shall be clearly demarcated
summary: Section 35(2) mandates that when multiple lead investment bankers are involved in an issue, the specific responsibilities of each must be clearly demarcated.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (3) An investment banker shall not undertake any activity, except for marketing of the issue or offer, if the investment banker is a promoter or an associate of the issuer of securities or of any person making an offer to sell or purchase securities in terms of any regulations made by the Authority
summary: Section 35(3) prohibits investment bankers from performing any activities other than marketing if they are a promoter or associate of the issuer or the person offering securities.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (4) No investment banker or any of its principal officer, directors, partner or manager shall either on their respective accounts or through their associates or relatives enter into any transaction in securities of issuer on the basis of unpublished price sensitive information obtained by them in
summary: Section 35(4) prohibits investment bankers, their officers, directors, partners, or managers from trading in securities based on unpublished price sensitive information obtained during professional assignments.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: price sensitive information obtained by them in the course of any professional assignment
summary: Section 35(4) extends the prohibition against trading on unpublished price sensitive information to the associates and relatives of the investment banker and its key personnel.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (5) An investment banker shall submit to the Authority complete particulars of any transaction for acquisition of securities of any body corporate whose issue is being managed by that investment banker within fifteen days from the date of entering into such transaction
summary: Section 35(5) requires investment bankers to report to the Authority all particulars of securities acquisitions in a body corporate whose issue they are managing within fifteen days of the transaction.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (6) An investment banker may act as an underwriter of an issue in an IFSC, subject to the following conditions: (a) An investment banker acting as an underwriter, shall enter into an agreement with the issuer of securities, on whose behalf it is acting as an underwriter, which shall have the
summary: Section 35(6)(a) mandates that an investment banker acting as an underwriter in an IFSC must execute a formal agreement with the issuer.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: is acting as an underwriter, which shall have the understanding in relation to amount of underwriting obligations and commission, allocation of duties and responsibilities, timelines and other relevant details
summary: The underwriting agreement must specify the underwriting obligations, commission, allocation of duties, responsibilities, and project timelines.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (b) At any point of time, the total underwriting obligations under all the agreements shall not exceed twenty times the net worth of the investment banker.
summary: Section 35(6)(b) limits the total underwriting obligations of an investment banker to a maximum of twenty times their net worth.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (1) A research entity shall have written internal policies and control procedures governing the dealing and trading by any employee for: (a) addressing actual or potential conflict of interest arising from such dealings or trading of securities of subject company; (b) promoting objective and
summary: Section 36(1) requires research entities to implement written internal policies governing employee securities trading to mitigate conflicts of interest.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: of subject company; (b) promoting objective and reliable research that reflects unbiased view of research analyst; and (c) preventing the use of research report or research analysis to manipulate the financial market
summary: Internal policies must promote objective, reliable, and unbiased research while preventing the manipulation of financial markets through research reports.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (2) A research entity shall ensure that the procedures or controls designed to manage actual or potential conflicts of interest are based on the nature, scale and complexity of the business
summary: Section 36(2) stipulates that conflict of interest controls must be proportionate to the nature, scale, and complexity of the research entity's business.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (3) The internal policy should ensure that the conflicts of interest are identified and adequately addressed so that the quality of the research report is not compromised
summary: Section 36(3) mandates that internal policies must identify and address conflicts of interest to maintain the integrity and quality of research reports.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (4) A research entity shall have in place appropriate mechanisms to ensure independence of its research activities from its other business activities. (5) A research entity shall establish, implement and enforce policies and procedures for personal trading by its analysts and their associates
summary: Section 36(4) and (5) require research entities to maintain independence between research and other business activities and enforce policies for personal trading by analysts.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (6) Personal trading activities of the individuals employed as research analyst by research entity shall be monitored, recorded and wherever necessary, shall be subject to a formal approval process
summary: Section 36(6) requires research entities to monitor, record, and implement a formal approval process for the personal trading activities of research analysts.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (7) The policy shall ensure that an analyst does not trade for himself in a manner that is contrary to his outstanding research recommendations, except in special circumstances, where the analyst shall be required to obtain prior written approval for each trade
summary: Section 36(7) prohibits analysts from trading contrary to their own research recommendations unless they obtain prior written approval under special circumstances.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (8) A research entity shall ensure that the remuneration of analysts is structured in a way to avoid any bias in his research analyses and recommendations
summary: Section 36(8) mandates that research entities structure analyst remuneration to prevent bias in research analyses and recommendations.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (9) A research entity shall ensure that there are robust and effective barriers between the entity and other business dealings of the entity or its group entity to ensure independence and objectivity of the research reports
summary: Section 36(9) requires research entities to maintain robust barriers between research operations and other business dealings to ensure the independence and objectivity of research reports.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (10) A research entity shall not provide any promise or assurance of favourable review in its research report to a company or industry or sector or group of companies or business group as consideration to commence or influence a business relationship or for the receipt of compensation or other
summary: Section 36(10) prohibits research entities from promising favorable reviews to companies or sectors as consideration for business relationships or compensation.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: or for the receipt of compensation or other benefits
summary: Section 36(10) extends the prohibition against promising favorable reviews to include the receipt of any benefits as consideration for business influence.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (11) A research entity shall ensure that the information provided in the report is complete, concise and specific such that investors can understand the actual or potential conflicts of interest and their likely impact on the quality of the research report published
summary: Section 36(11) requires research reports to contain complete, concise, and specific information regarding actual or potential conflicts of interest and their impact on report quality.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (12) A research entity shall disclose any material interest in the report that may create a potential conflict of interest and thereby affect the ability of the entity to maintain independence and objectivity: Explanation: The research entity shall disclose in the report if the research entity or
summary: Section 36(12) mandates the disclosure of any material interest in a report that could compromise the research entity's independence or objectivity.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: disclose in the report if the research entity or the research analyst or his associate or his relative has: (a) any material financial interest in the subject company and the nature of such financial interest; (b) beneficial ownership of one or more per cent
summary: Section 36(12) requires disclosure of material financial interests and beneficial ownership of one percent or more of subject company securities by the entity, analyst, associates, or relatives.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: of the securities of the subject company; (c) any material business relationship with the subject company over the past 12 months that may result in conflict of interest; and (d) any other material conflict of interest relating to the subject company
summary: Section 36(12) requires disclosure of any material business relationships with the subject company within the past 12 months and any other material conflicts of interest.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (13) A research entity shall take steps to ensure that facts in its research reports are based on reliable information and shall define the terms used in making recommendations, and these terms shall be consistently used
summary: Section 36(13) requires research entities to base reports on reliable information and consistently define all terms used in making recommendations.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (14) A research entity shall have adequate documentary basis, supported by research, for preparing a research report. (15) Where a research entity employs a rating system, it must clearly define the meaning of each such rating including the time horizon and benchmarks on which a rating is based.
summary: Section 36(14) and (15) mandate that research reports be supported by adequate documentary evidence and that any rating system must clearly define meanings, time horizons, and benchmarks.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter IV - SPECIFIC OBLIGATIONS AND RESPONSIBILITES

content: (1) The Authority may suo motu or upon receipt of information or complaint at any time appoint one or more persons as inspecting authority to undertake the inspection of the books, accounts, records, documents, infrastructure, procedures and systems of a CMI, for any purpose, including the purposes
summary: Section 37(1) empowers the Authority to appoint an inspecting authority, either suo motu or upon complaint, to inspect the books, records, infrastructure, and systems of a CMI.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter V - Inspection

content: of a CMI, for any purpose, including the purposes as specified under sub-regulation (2)
summary: This segment clarifies that the inspection of a CMI under Section 37(1) may be conducted for any purpose specified in sub-regulation (2).

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter V - Inspection

content: (2) The purposes referred to in sub-regulation (1) may include, - (a) to ensure that the books of account, records and documents are being maintained in the manner as required under these regulations; (b) to ensure that the provisions of the Act, the regulations and circulars made thereunder, are
summary: Section 37(2)(a)-(b) defines the inspection purposes as verifying the maintenance of books of account and ensuring compliance with the Act, regulations, and circulars.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter V - Inspection

content: regulations and circulars made thereunder, are complied with; (c) to ascertain whether adequate internal control systems, procedures and safeguards have been established or are being followed by the CMI to fulfil its obligations under these regulations; (d) to ascertain whether any circumstances
summary: Section 37(2)(c) authorizes inspections to evaluate the adequacy and implementation of internal control systems, procedures, and safeguards within a CMI.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter V - Inspection

content: (d) to ascertain whether any circumstances exist which would render the intermediary unfit or ineligible; (e) to inquire into the complaints received from the investors, clients, other market participants, or any other person on any matter having a bearing on the activities of the intermediary; and
summary: Section 37(2)(d)-(e) permits inspections to determine if a CMI remains fit or eligible and to investigate complaints from investors, clients, or market participants.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter V - Inspection

content: on the activities of the intermediary; and (f) to inquire suo motu into such matters as may be deemed fit in the interest of investors or the capital market in an IFSC
summary: Section 37(2)(f) grants the Authority power to conduct suo motu inquiries into matters deemed necessary for the protection of investors or the integrity of the IFSC capital market.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter V - Inspection

content: (3) Before undertaking an inspection under sub-regulation (1), the inspecting authority shall give a notice to the CMI: Provided that where the inspecting authority is satisfied that in the interest of the investors no such notice should be given, it may, for reasons to be recorded in writing,
summary: Section 37(3) mandates that the inspecting authority provide prior notice to the CMI before commencing an inspection, unless specific conditions warrant otherwise.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter V - Inspection

content: it may, for reasons to be recorded in writing, dispense with such notice
summary: Under the proviso to Section 37(3), the inspecting authority may dispense with the notice requirement if it records written reasons justifying the action in the interest of investors.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter V - Inspection

content: (4) Notwithstanding anything contained in sub-regulations (1), (2) and (3), a recognised stock exchange, a recognised clearing corporation or a recognised depository may conduct inspection of a broker dealer (trading member), a clearing member or a depository participant respectively, in accordance
summary: Section 37(4) preserves the authority of recognised stock exchanges, clearing corporations, and depositories to inspect their respective members in accordance with their own bye-laws.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter V - Inspection

content: participant respectively, in accordance with its bye-laws
summary: This segment confirms that inspections by recognised market infrastructure institutions of their members are governed by their respective bye-laws as per Section 37(4).

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter V - Inspection

content: (5) The Authority and the relevant market infrastructure institution may conduct joint inspection of a CMI.
summary: Section 37(5) authorizes the Authority and the relevant market infrastructure institution to conduct joint inspections of a Clearing Member Intermediary (CMI).

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter V - Inspection

content: (1) Where an inspection of a CMI is undertaken by the Authority, such CMI and every principal officer, proprietor, partner, designated partner, trustee, director, chairperson, officer, employee and any agent of the intermediary shall provide all assistance and cooperate with the inspection
summary: Section 38(1) mandates that a CMI and its principal officers, partners, directors, employees, and agents must provide full assistance and cooperation during an inspection conducted by the Authority.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter V - Inspection

content: all assistance and cooperate with the inspection authority and shall furnish books of accounts, records and documents to the inspection authority with such statements and information relating to its activities within such time as decided by the inspection authority
summary: Under Section 38, the CMI must furnish all requested books of accounts, records, documents, and statements to the inspection authority within the specified timeframe.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter V - Inspection

content: (2) The CMI shall give all assistance as may be required in connection with the inspection and allow the inspecting authority to have reasonable access to its premises and extend reasonable facility for examining any books of accounts, records and documents in its possession, and also provide
summary: Section 38(2) requires the CMI to grant the inspecting authority reasonable access to its premises and facilities for the examination of books, records, and documents.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter V - Inspection

content: and documents in its possession, and also provide copies of records or documents or other material which in the opinion of the inspecting authority are relevant for the purposes of the inspection
summary: Section 38(2) obligates the CMI to provide copies of records, documents, or other materials deemed relevant by the inspecting authority for the purposes of the inspection.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter V - Inspection

content: (3) The Inspecting Authority, in the course of inspection, shall be entitled to examine or record the statements of any principal officer, partner, designated partner, trustee, director, chairperson, officer, employee and any agent of the CMI
summary: Section 38(3) empowers the inspecting authority to examine or record the statements of any principal officer, partner, director, employee, or agent of the CMI during an inspection.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter V - Inspection

content: (4) It shall be the duty of every principal officer, partner, designated partner, trustee, director, chairperson, CEO, KMPs, officer, employee and any agent of the CMI to give to the inspecting officer all assistance in connection with the inspection, which the inspecting officer may require.
summary: Section 38(4) imposes a mandatory duty on all CMI personnel, including CEOs and Key Managerial Personnel (KMPs), to provide all necessary assistance required by the inspecting officer.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter V - Inspection

content: (1) The Authority may appoint any person to inspect the books of account, records, documents infrastructures, systems and procedures or affairs of a CMI: Provided that such person so appointed shall have the same powers of an inspecting authority: Provided further that a CMI and its employees shall
summary: Section 39(1) allows the Authority to appoint external persons to inspect a CMI's books, records, systems, and affairs, granting such appointees the full powers of an inspecting authority.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter V - Inspection

content: further that a CMI and its employees shall have the same obligations towards such person, so appointed as they have towards inspecting authority as specified in regulation 38 with respect to such inspection
summary: Section 39(1) stipulates that a CMI and its employees owe the same obligations to an externally appointed inspector as they do to the Authority under Section 38.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter V - Inspection

content: (2) The Authority shall be entitled to recover expenses, including fees paid to such person, relating to such inspection from the CMI.
summary: Section 39(2) entitles the Authority to recover all inspection-related expenses, including fees paid to appointed inspectors, directly from the CMI.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter V - Inspection

content: The inspecting authority shall submit an inspection report including interim reports to the Authority, and the Authority may take such action as it may deem fit and appropriate on such report.
summary: Section 40 mandates that the inspecting authority submit inspection and interim reports to the Authority, which retains the power to take appropriate action based on these findings.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter V - Inspection

content: (1) The Authority may take such action as deemed fit, including suspension or cancellation of registration, against a CMI if it: (a) fails to comply with any conditions subject to which a certificate of registration has been granted; (b) contravenes any of the provisions of the Act or any rules,
summary: Under Section 41(1), the Authority may suspend or cancel the registration of a Capital Market Intermediary (CMI) for failing to comply with registration conditions or contravening the Act, rules, regulations, or circulars.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Miscellaneous

content: any of the provisions of the Act or any rules, regulations, notifications, circulars, guidelines, directions, instructions issued under the Act or any other relevant statute; (c) fails to furnish any information relating to its activity as a capital market intermediary as directed by the Authority;
summary: Section 41(1)(b)-(c) authorizes the Authority to take action against a CMI for violating any Act, regulation, or guideline, or for failing to furnish required information regarding its activities.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Miscellaneous

content: market intermediary as directed by the Authority; (d) furnishes to the Authority information which is false or misleading in any material particular; (e) does not submit periodic returns or reports as directed by the Authority; (f) does not co-operate in any enquiry, inspection or investigation
summary: Section 41(1)(d)-(f) empowers the Authority to penalize a CMI for providing false or misleading information, failing to submit periodic reports, or refusing to cooperate with enquiries, inspections, or investigations.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Miscellaneous

content: in any enquiry, inspection or investigation conducted by the Authority; (g) fails to resolve the complaints of investors or fails to give a satisfactory reply to the Authority; or (h) commits any other act/omission which in the opinion of the Authority warrants such action or which is against the
summary: Section 41(1)(g)-(h) allows the Authority to take action against a CMI for failing to resolve investor complaints, providing unsatisfactory replies, or committing acts detrimental to investor interests.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Miscellaneous

content: warrants such action or which is against the interest of the investors
summary: Section 41(1)(h) establishes that the Authority may take disciplinary action against a CMI for any act or omission deemed contrary to the interests of investors.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Miscellaneous

content: (2) Without prejudice to sub-regulation (1), a recognised stock exchange may take such action as deemed fit, including suspension, against a broker dealer (trading member), in accordance with the applicable laws
summary: Section 41(2) grants a recognised stock exchange the authority to take disciplinary measures, including suspension, against a broker dealer (trading member) in accordance with applicable laws.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Miscellaneous

content: (3) Without prejudice to sub-regulation (1), a recognised clearing corporation may take such action as deemed fit, including suspension, against a clearing member, in accordance with the applicable laws
summary: Section 41(3) permits a recognised clearing corporation to take disciplinary action, including suspension, against a clearing member pursuant to applicable laws.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Miscellaneous

content: (4) Without prejudice to sub-regulation (1), a recognised depository may take such action as deemed fit, including suspension, against a depository participant, in accordance with the applicable laws.
summary: Section 41(4) authorizes a recognised depository to take disciplinary action, including suspension, against a depository participant in accordance with applicable laws.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Miscellaneous

content: The Authority may call for any information, documents or records from a CMI.
summary: Section 42 empowers the Authority to demand the production of any information, documents, or records from a Capital Market Intermediary (CMI).

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Miscellaneous

content: In order to remove any difficulties in the interpretation or application of the provisions of these regulations, the Authority may issue directions through guidance notes or circulars.
summary: Section 43 empowers the Authority to issue guidance notes or circulars to resolve difficulties in the interpretation or application of these regulations.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Miscellaneous

content: (1) The Authority may, in the interest of development and regulation of financial services market in IFSC, relax the strict enforcement of any requirements of these regulations
summary: Section 44(1) grants the Authority the power to relax strict enforcement of regulatory requirements to facilitate the development and regulation of the financial services market in the IFSC.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Miscellaneous

content: (2) For seeking relaxation under sub-regulation (1), an application, giving details and the grounds on which such relaxation has been sought, shall be filed with the Authority along with a non-refundable fee as may be specified by the Authority
summary: Section 44(2) mandates that applicants seeking relaxation must submit a formal application detailing the grounds for the request along with a specified non-refundable fee.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Miscellaneous

content: (3) The Authority shall process such application within thirty days of the date of receipt of the application complete in all respects and shall record reasons for acceptance or refusal of the relaxations sought by the applicant.
summary: Section 44(3) requires the Authority to process relaxation applications within thirty days of receipt and mandates the recording of reasons for any acceptance or refusal.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Miscellaneous

content: For the purposes of implementation of these regulations and matters incidental thereto, the Authority may specify norms, procedures, processes, additional requirements etc. by way of circulars or guidelines or directions.
summary: Section 45 authorizes the Authority to specify norms, procedures, processes, and additional requirements through circulars, guidelines, or directions for the implementation of these regulations.

metadata.contained_chapter: Chapter VI - Miscellaneous

content: The powers exercisable by the Authority under these regulations shall also be exercisable by any officer of the Authority to whom such powers are delegated by the Authority.
summary: Section 46 permits the delegation of the Authority's powers under these regulations to any of its officers.
