metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule I

content: SCHEDULE II CODE OF CONDUCT Part A - Code of Conduct - All capital market intermediaries (as applicable) 1
summary: Schedule II, Part A establishes the Code of Conduct applicable to all capital market intermediaries (CMIs).

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: A CMI shall make all efforts to protect the interest of investors and render the best possible advice to the clients having regard to the needs of the clients, environment and its own professional skills. 2
summary: CMIs are required to protect investor interests and provide advice tailored to client needs, the environment, and professional skills.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 2. A CMI shall in the conduct of its business, observe high standards of integrity and fairness and fulfil its obligations in a prompt, ethical and professional manner. 3. A CMI shall at all times exercise due diligence, ensure proper care and exercise independent professional judgment. 4
summary: CMIs must maintain high standards of integrity, fairness, and ethics, while exercising due diligence and independent professional judgment in all business conduct.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 4. A CMI shall not indulge in manipulative, fraudulent or deceptive transactions or schemes or spread rumours with a view to distorting market equilibrium or making personal gains. 5
summary: CMIs are prohibited from engaging in manipulative, fraudulent, or deceptive transactions and from spreading rumors to distort market equilibrium or secure personal gain.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 5. A CMI shall not create false market either singly or in collusion with other intermediaries or the issuer in a manner that is detrimental to the interests of investors, or which leads to interference with the fair and smooth functioning of the market. 6
summary: CMIs must not create false markets, either individually or through collusion, that interfere with fair market functioning or harm investor interests.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 6. A CMI shall endeavour to ensure that inquires and grievances of the investors are dealt with in a timely and appropriate manner. 7
summary: CMIs are obligated to ensure that investor inquiries and grievances are addressed in a timely and appropriate manner.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 7. A CMI shall not make any exaggerated statement either oral or in written form to the client about its capability, qualification and achievement in regard to services rendered to the client. 8
summary: Clause 7 prohibits a Capital Market Intermediary (CMI) from making exaggerated oral or written statements regarding its capabilities, qualifications, or achievements to clients.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 8. A CMI shall maintain confidentiality with respect to the information about its clients, except where such disclosures are required to be made in compliance with any law for the time being in force. 9
summary: Clause 8 mandates that a CMI maintain client confidentiality, permitting disclosure only when required by applicable law.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 9. A CMI shall avoid conflict of interest and make adequate disclosure of its possible conflict of interest and duties and shall put in place a mechanism to resolve any conflict of interest situation. 10
summary: Clause 9 requires a CMI to avoid conflicts of interest, provide adequate disclosure of potential conflicts, and establish mechanisms for their resolution.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 10. A CMI shall not indulge in any unfair competition, which is likely to harm the interests of other capital market intermediaries and investors. 11. A CMI shall not discriminate amongst its clients, save and except on ethical and commercial considerations. 12
summary: Clause 10 and 11 prohibit a CMI from engaging in unfair competition and mandate non-discrimination among clients, except based on ethical or commercial considerations.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 12. A CMI shall ensure that any change in registration status/any penal action taken by Authority or any material change in financials which may adversely affect the interests of clients/investors is promptly informed to the clients. 13
summary: Clause 12 requires a CMI to promptly notify clients of any changes in registration status, penal actions by the Authority, or material financial changes affecting client interests.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 13. A CMI shall inform the Authority promptly about any action initiated against it in respect of material breach or non-compliance of any law, regulations and direction issued by the Authority or any other regulatory body. 14
summary: Clause 13 mandates that a CMI promptly inform the Authority of any actions initiated against it for material breaches or non-compliance with laws, regulations, or regulatory directions.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 14. A CMI shall ensure that it and any of its employees shall not render, directly or indirectly any investment advice about any security in the publicly accessible media, unless a disclosure of its interest in the said security has been made while rendering such advice. 15
summary: Clause 14 prohibits a CMI and its employees from providing investment advice on securities in public media without disclosing any personal interest in those securities.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 15. A CMI shall ensure that it or any of its principal officer, directors, or employees having power of management shall not indulge in insider trading. 16
summary: Clause 15 prohibits a CMI, its principal officers, directors, and management-level employees from engaging in insider trading.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 16. A CMI shall have and employ effectively appropriate resources and procedures which are needed for the efficient performance of its business activities. 17
summary: Clause 16 requires a CMI to employ appropriate resources and procedures to ensure the efficient performance of its business activities.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 17. A CMI shall have internal control procedures and financial and operational capabilities adequate enough to protect the clients and investors from financial loss arising from theft, fraud, omissions and professional misconduct. 18
summary: Clause 17 mandates that a CMI maintain internal control procedures and sufficient financial and operational capabilities to protect clients from losses due to theft, fraud, omissions, or professional misconduct.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 18. A CMI shall have an internal policy for outsourcing of its activities from outside of IFSC and the CMI shall ensure compliance with the policy at all times. 19. A CMI shall develop its own internal code of conduct for governing its internal operations and conduct of its employees. 20
summary: CMIs must maintain and comply with internal policies for outsourcing activities outside the IFSC and establish an internal code of conduct for operations and employee behavior.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 20. A CMI shall ensure that the compliance officer has adequate freedom and power for effective discharge of his duties. 21. A CMI shall ensure that any person it employs or appoints is a fit and proper person and otherwise qualified to act in the capacity so employed or appointed. 22
summary: CMIs must grant the compliance officer sufficient authority for duty discharge and ensure all personnel are 'fit and proper' and qualified for their roles.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 22. A CMI shall not be a party to or instrumental for: (a) creation of false market; (b) price rigging or manipulation; (c) passing of unpublished price sensitive information to any other intermediary or any person, in respect of any securities which are listed and proposed to be listed in any
summary: CMIs are prohibited from engaging in market manipulation, price rigging, or the dissemination of unpublished price-sensitive information regarding listed or proposed securities.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: which are listed and proposed to be listed in any stock exchange
summary: This provision applies to securities that are listed or proposed to be listed on any stock exchange.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 23. The senior management of a CMI shall bear primary responsibility for ensuring the maintenance of appropriate standards of conduct and adherence to proper procedures by the intermediary. 24
summary: Senior management of a CMI holds primary responsibility for maintaining conduct standards and ensuring adherence to established procedures.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 24. A CMI shall not make untrue statement or suppress any material fact in any documents, reports or information furnished to the Authority. 25
summary: CMIs are prohibited from making untrue statements or suppressing material facts in any documents, reports, or information submitted to the Authority.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 25. A CMI shall not make a recommendation to any client or investor who may be expected to rely thereon to acquire, dispose of or retain any securities unless he has reasonable grounds to believe that the recommendation is suitable
summary: CMIs must not provide investment recommendations to clients unless they have reasonable grounds to believe the recommendation is suitable for the client.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: Part B In addition to compliance with the code of conduct as specified in Part A of this Schedule, the capital market intermediaries shall abide by the following code of conduct: A. Broker dealers / Clearing Members 1
summary: Part B mandates that capital market intermediaries, specifically broker dealers and clearing members, must adhere to additional code of conduct requirements beyond those in Part A.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: Broker dealers / Clearing Members 1. A broker dealer shall abide by all the provisions of the Act and the rules, regulations and byelaws issued by the Government of India, the Authority and the recognised stock exchange from time to time as may be applicable. 2
summary: Broker dealers are required to comply with the Act, rules, regulations, and bye-laws issued by the Government of India, the Authority, and recognized stock exchanges.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 2. A clearing member shall abide by all the provisions of the Act and the rules, regulations and bye-laws issued by the Government of India, the Authority and the recognised clearing corporation from time to time as may be applicable. 3
summary: Clearing members must comply with the Act, rules, regulations, and bye-laws issued by the Government of India, the Authority, and recognized clearing corporations.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 3. A broker dealer shall not involve itself in excessive speculative business in the market beyond reasonable levels not commensurate with its financial soundness. 4
summary: Broker dealers are prohibited from engaging in excessive speculative business that exceeds levels commensurate with their financial soundness.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 4. A broker dealer shall faithfully execute the orders for buying and selling of securities at the best available price and not refuse to deal with a small investor merely on the ground of the volume of business involved. 5
summary: Broker dealers must execute securities orders at the best available price and are prohibited from refusing small investors based solely on transaction volume.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 5. A broker dealer shall promptly inform its client about the execution or non-execution of an order, and make prompt payment in respect of securities sold and arrange for prompt delivery of securities purchased by its clients. 6
summary: Broker dealers must promptly notify clients regarding order execution status and ensure timely payment for securities sold and delivery for securities purchased.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 6. A broker dealer shall issue without delay to its client a contract note for all transactions in the form specified by the recognised stock exchange. 7. A broker dealer shall not encourage sales or purchases of securities with the sole object of generating brokerage or commission. 8
summary: Broker dealers must issue contract notes in the prescribed format without delay and are prohibited from churning accounts to generate brokerage or commission.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 8. A broker dealer shall not furnish false or misleading quotations or give any other false or misleading advice or information to the clients with a view of inducing them to do business in particular securities and enabling itself to earn brokerage or commission thereby. 9
summary: Broker dealers are prohibited from providing false or misleading information or quotations to induce clients into transactions for the purpose of earning commissions.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 9. A broker dealer shall not deal or transact business knowingly, directly or indirectly or execute an order for a client who has failed to carry out its commitments in relation to securities with another broker dealer. 10
summary: Broker dealers are prohibited from transacting business for clients who have defaulted on commitments with other broker dealers.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 10. A broker dealer shall not resort to unfair means of inducing clients from other broker dealers. B. Credit rating agencies 1
summary: Broker dealers are prohibited from using unfair methods to solicit or induce clients away from other broker dealers.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: B. Credit rating agencies 1. A registered credit rating agency shall at all times exercise due diligence, ensure proper care and exercise independent professional judgment in order to achieve and maintain objectivity and independence in the rating process. 2
summary: Registered credit rating agencies must exercise due diligence, proper care, and independent professional judgment to maintain objectivity and independence in the rating process.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 2. A registered credit rating agency shall maintain an arm’s length relationship between the credit rating activity and its other activities. 3
summary: Registered credit rating agencies are required to maintain an arm's length relationship between their credit rating activities and all other business operations.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 3. A registered credit rating agency shall have a reasonable and adequate basis for performing rating evaluations, with the support of appropriate and in-depth rating researches. It shall also maintain records to support its decisions. 4
summary: Registered credit rating agencies must base rating evaluations on adequate, in-depth research and maintain comprehensive records to support all rating decisions.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 4. A registered credit rating agency shall have in place a rating process that reflects consistent and international rating standards. 5. A registered credit rating agency shall disclose its rating methodology to clients, users and the public. 6
summary: Registered credit rating agencies must implement rating processes aligned with consistent international standards and publicly disclose their rating methodologies to clients and users.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 6. A registered credit rating agency shall not indulge in any unfair competition. 7. A registered credit rating agency shall keep track of all important changes relating to its client companies and shall develop efficient and responsive systems to yield timely and accurate ratings
summary: Registered credit rating agencies are prohibited from engaging in unfair competition and must maintain efficient systems to track client changes for the delivery of timely, accurate ratings.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: Further a credit rating agency shall also monitor closely all relevant factors that might affect the creditworthiness of the issuers. 8
summary: Credit rating agencies are mandated to continuously monitor all relevant factors impacting the creditworthiness of issuers.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 8. A registered credit rating agency shall, wherever necessary, disclose to its clients, possible sources of conflict of duties and interests, which could impair their ability to make fair, objective and unbiased ratings
summary: Registered credit rating agencies must disclose potential conflicts of duties and interests to clients to ensure the integrity, objectivity, and unbiased nature of their ratings.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: Further, a registered credit rating agency shall ensure that no conflict of interest exists between any member of the rating committee participating in the rating analysis, and that of its clients. 9
summary: Registered credit rating agencies must prevent conflicts of interest between rating committee members and the agency's clients during the rating analysis process.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 9. A registered credit rating agency shall ensure that there is no misuse of any privileged information including prior knowledge of rating decisions or changes. 10
summary: Registered credit rating agencies must prohibit the misuse of privileged information, specifically regarding prior knowledge of rating decisions or subsequent changes.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 10. A registered credit rating agency shall develop its own internal code of conduct for governing its internal operations and laying down standards of appropriate conduct for its employees and officers in the carrying out of their duties within the credit rating agency and as a part of the industry
summary: Registered credit rating agencies are required to establish an internal code of conduct to govern operations and define professional standards for employees and officers.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: C. Custodians 1. A custodian shall be prompt in distributing dividends, interest or any such accruals of income received or collected by it on behalf of its clients on the securities held in custody. 2
summary: Custodians must promptly distribute all dividends, interest, and income accruals collected on behalf of clients for securities held in custody.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 2. A custodian shall be continuously accountable for the movement of securities or financial products in and out of the custody account, deposit, and withdrawal of cash from the client's account and shall provide complete audit trail, whenever called for by the client or the Authority. 3
summary: Custodians must maintain continuous accountability for all security movements and cash transactions, providing a complete audit trail upon request by clients or the Authority.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 3. A custodian shall establish and maintain adequate infrastructural facility to be able to discharge custodial services to the satisfaction of clients, and the operating procedures and systems of the custodian shall be well documented and backed by operations manuals. 4
summary: Custodians must maintain adequate infrastructural facilities and documented operating procedures, supported by operations manuals, to ensure the effective delivery of custodial services.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 4. A custodian shall take precautions necessary to ensure that continuity in record keeping is not lost or destroyed and that sufficient back up of records is available. 5
summary: A custodian must implement necessary precautions to ensure record-keeping continuity and maintain sufficient backups to prevent loss or destruction of records.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 5. A custodian shall create and maintain the records of securities held in custody in such manner that the tracing of securities or obtaining duplicate title documents is facilitated, in the event of loss of original records for any reason. 6
summary: A custodian is required to maintain records of securities in a manner that facilitates tracing or obtaining duplicate title documents if original records are lost.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 6. A custodian shall extend to other custodial entities, depositories and clearing organizations all such co-operation that is necessary for the conduct of business in the areas of inter custodial settlements, transfer of securities and transfer of funds. 7
summary: A custodian must cooperate with other custodial entities, depositories, and clearing organizations to facilitate inter-custodial settlements and the transfer of securities and funds.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 7. A custodian shall ensure that an arm’s length relationship is maintained, both in terms of staff and systems, from its other businesses. 8. A custodian shall exercise due diligence in safe-keeping and administration of the assets of its clients. D. Debenture trustees 1
summary: A custodian must maintain an arm's length relationship regarding staff and systems from other business operations and exercise due diligence in the safekeeping and administration of client assets.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: D. Debenture trustees 1. A registered debenture trustee shall ensure that adequate disclosures are made to the debenture holders, in a comprehensible and timely manner so as to enable them to make a balanced and informed decision. 2
summary: A registered debenture trustee must provide adequate, comprehensible, and timely disclosures to debenture holders to facilitate informed decision-making.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 2. A registered debenture trustee shall take all reasonable steps to establish the true and full identity of each of its clients, and of each client’s financial situation and maintain record of the same. 3
summary: A registered debenture trustee is obligated to verify and maintain records of the true identity and financial situation of each client.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 3. A registered debenture trustee shall share information available with it regarding client companies with credit rating agencies, wherever required. 4
summary: A registered debenture trustee must share information regarding client companies with credit rating agencies when required.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 4. A registered debenture trustee shall make reasonable efforts to avoid misrepresentation and ensure that the information provided to the debenture holders is not misleading. E. Depository Participants 1
summary: A registered debenture trustee must make reasonable efforts to prevent misrepresentation and ensure that all information provided to debenture holders is not misleading.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: E. Depository Participants 1. A depository participant shall not increase charges/fees for the services rendered without proper advance notice of at least seven (7) days to the beneficial owners. 2
summary: A depository participant is prohibited from increasing service charges or fees without providing at least seven days' advance notice to beneficial owners.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 2. A depository participant shall be prompt and diligent in opening of a beneficial owner account, dispatch of the dematerialisation request form, rematerialisation request form and execution of debit instruction slip and in all the other activities undertaken by them on behalf of the beneficial
summary: A depository participant must act with promptness and diligence in opening beneficial owner accounts, processing dematerialisation and rematerialisation requests, and executing debit instruction slips.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: undertaken by them on behalf of the beneficial owners
summary: Defines actions performed by agents or intermediaries on behalf of beneficial owners.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 3. A depository participant shall take adequate and necessary steps to ensure that continuity in data and record keeping is maintained and that the data or records are not lost or destroyed. It shall also ensure that for electronic records and data, up-to-date back up is always available with it
summary: Depository participants must maintain data continuity, prevent record loss or destruction, and ensure up-to-date electronic backups are consistently available.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

summary: Numerical reference indicating the start of a new regulatory provision.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 4. A depository participant shall ensure that it has satisfactory internal control procedures in place as well as adequate financial and operational capabilities which can be reasonably expected to take care of any losses arising due to theft, fraud and other dishonest acts, professional misconduct
summary: Depository participants are required to maintain internal control procedures and sufficient financial and operational capabilities to mitigate losses from theft, fraud, or professional misconduct.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: and other dishonest acts, professional misconduct or omissions
summary: Extends the scope of liability for depository participants to include losses resulting from omissions and other dishonest acts.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: F. Distributors 1. A registered distributor shall avoid malpractices, such as mis-selling of capital market products and services, and shall consider clients’ interest and suitability to their financial needs. 2
summary: Registered distributors must avoid malpractices like mis-selling and ensure capital market products align with client interests and financial suitability.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 2. When dealing with clients other than sophisticated investors, a registered distributor shall undertake due diligence of capital market products and services being distributed to them, and also assess the suitability of product / service to the investors
summary: Registered distributors must conduct due diligence on capital market products and assess their suitability for non-sophisticated investors.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: For this purpose, a distributor shall seek information from such clients about their financial status, investment experience, investment objectives, etc. to be in a better position to offer them such capital market products and services which are suitable to their risk profile. 3
summary: Distributors must collect client information regarding financial status, investment experience, and objectives to ensure product recommendations match the client's risk profile.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 3. A registered distributor shall act in the best interests of the clients and the integrity of the market. A distributor shall inform the client if the capital market product or service is not deemed suitable for them. 4
summary: Registered distributors must prioritize client interests and market integrity, including the obligation to notify clients if a product or service is unsuitable.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 4. A registered distributor shall be fully conversant with the terms of the private placement memorandum, disclosure document and all other relevant agreements / documents. 5
summary: Registered distributors are required to be fully conversant with all relevant documentation, including private placement memoranda and disclosure documents.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 5. Wherever the client is a sophisticated investor, a registered distributor shall obtain a declaration to the effect that the investor understands the risks associated to the capital market product or service being distributed. 6
summary: Section 5 mandates that registered distributors obtain a declaration from sophisticated investors confirming their understanding of risks associated with specific capital market products or services.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 6. A registered distributor shall disclose all material information to its prospective clients, including but not limited to its business, disciplinary history, terms and conditions of distribution services, conflict of interest, affiliations with other intermediaries and any other material
summary: Section 6 requires registered distributors to disclose all material information to prospective clients, including business details, disciplinary history, service terms, conflicts of interest, and intermediary affiliations.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: with other intermediaries and any other material information
summary: This fragment pertains to the disclosure obligations of registered distributors regarding affiliations with other intermediaries and general material information.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

summary: This index entry represents the numerical heading for Section 7 of the schedule.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 7. A registered distributor shall urge its clients to go through the private placement memorandum, disclosure document and other applicable capital market product or service related documents, as the case may be, and agreement to be entered with the client and the regulated financial entities
summary: Section 7 mandates that registered distributors urge clients to review private placement memoranda, disclosure documents, and agreements with regulated financial entities before finalizing investment decisions.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: the client and the regulated financial entities before making the final decision
summary: This fragment specifies that clients must review agreements with regulated financial entities prior to making final investment decisions.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 8. A registered distributor shall disclose all material information regarding the capital market products and services being distributed to its clients, including related party transactions and self-positions
summary: Section 8 requires registered distributors to disclose all material information regarding capital market products and services, specifically including related party transactions and self-positions.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: If requested by a client, the distributor shall disclose the amount of direct and indirect remuneration and the basis of such remuneration it receives as a result of rendering distributing services to that client and whether there is any relation between the distributor and the entity offering the
summary: Upon client request, distributors must disclose the amount and basis of direct or indirect remuneration received for distribution services and any existing relationship with the product or service provider.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: the distributor and the entity offering the capital market product or service
summary: This fragment concerns the disclosure of the relationship between the distributor and the entity offering the capital market product or service.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 9. A registered distributor shall assist its clients in completing KYC and other related procedures and assist in compliance with relevant laws relating to, inter alia, AML and CFT to the extent applicable. 10
summary: Section 9 requires registered distributors to assist clients with KYC procedures and ensure compliance with applicable AML and CFT laws.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 10. A registered distributor shall abstain from tampering with the application form and other documents submitted by the client, including inserting, deleting, or changing any information in the application form or any other document provided by the client. 11
summary: Clause 10 prohibits registered distributors from tampering with client application forms or documents, specifically forbidding the insertion, deletion, or alteration of any provided information.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 11. A registered distributor shall provide to its clients full and latest information about the capital market products and services offered by the regulated financial entities and shall clearly highlight the assumptions made in performance calculations, risk assessments, performance projections etc
summary: Clause 11 mandates that registered distributors provide clients with current information on capital market products and services, requiring clear disclosure of assumptions regarding performance projections, risk assessments, and calculations.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 12. A registered distributor shall abstain from giving any assurance or cause any misrepresentation to its clients with respect to returns or risk characteristics of a capital market product or service. 13
summary: Clause 12 prohibits registered distributors from providing assurances or making misrepresentations to clients concerning the risk characteristics or potential returns of capital market products or services.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 13. A registered distributor shall abstain from attracting clients through offer of rebate, kickback, gifts, etc. 14
summary: Clause 13 prohibits registered distributors from using rebates, kickbacks, or gifts as inducements to attract clients.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 14. A registered distributor shall maintain necessary infrastructure to provide support to its clients and regulated financial entities, so as to be able to satisfactorily discharge its responsibilities as per the mutually agreed terms. 15
summary: Clause 14 requires registered distributors to maintain sufficient infrastructure to support clients and regulated financial entities, ensuring the satisfactory discharge of contractual responsibilities.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 15. A registered distributor shall ensure clear segregation of its proprietary investments and those carried out as part of distribution activities. If allowed to facilitate clients’ investments through omnibus structure, it shall ensure compliance with all applicable norms. 16
summary: Clause 15 mandates the segregation of a registered distributor's proprietary investments from distribution activities and requires compliance with applicable norms when utilizing an omnibus structure for client investments.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 16. A registered distributor shall maintain adequate records in relation to its clients, whether in physical or digital form including correspondence with the clients on particular capital market product or service suitability and consent/dissent of the clients, wherever applicable. 17
summary: Clause 16 requires registered distributors to maintain comprehensive physical or digital records of client correspondence, including documentation regarding product suitability and client consent or dissent.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 17. A registered distributor shall ensure that all client related statutory communications as well as such other reports as mutually agreed are reliably and timely sent to its clients. 18
summary: Clause 17 mandates that registered distributors ensure the reliable and timely delivery of all statutory communications and mutually agreed reports to their clients.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 18. A registered distributor shall take all reasonable steps to avoid conflicts of interest (whether actual or perceived) and develop appropriate policies and procedures to identify, manage, monitor and, where applicable, disclose, those conflicts of interest in order to prevent them from adversely
summary: Clause 18 requires registered distributors to implement policies and procedures to identify, manage, monitor, and disclose actual or perceived conflicts of interest to protect client interests.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: interest in order to prevent them from adversely affecting the interests of the clients
summary: This provision serves as a continuation of Clause 18, emphasizing the obligation to prevent conflicts of interest from adversely affecting the interests of the clients.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 19. In order to avoid conflict of interest arising due to multiple activities, a registered distributor shall ensure segregation of the activities and proper disclosures about segregation to the clients. 20
summary: Clause 19 mandates that a registered distributor must segregate multiple business activities to prevent conflicts of interest and provide clear disclosures regarding such segregation to clients.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 20. A registered distributor shall maintain and protect confidentiality of its clients’ details, deals and transactions, investment goals which it comes to know in the course of business relationship. 21
summary: Clause 20 requires a registered distributor to maintain and protect the confidentiality of client details, transactions, and investment goals acquired during the business relationship.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 21. A registered distributor shall have a robust complaint redressal mechanism and an escalation matrix. It shall endeavour to resolve all grievances / complaints arising out of its distribution activities in a time bound manner. 22
summary: Clause 21 obligates a registered distributor to implement a robust complaint redressal mechanism and escalation matrix to resolve grievances arising from distribution activities in a time-bound manner.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 22. A registered distributor shall abstain from encouraging over transacting and churning of portfolio of the clients to earn higher remuneration. 23
summary: Clause 22 prohibits a registered distributor from encouraging over-transacting or portfolio churning for the purpose of generating higher remuneration.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 23. When distributing various capital market products and services, a registered distributor shall ensure that clients’ interest are paramount and that earning extra remuneration should never form the basis for distributing any product or service to its clients. 24
summary: Clause 23 stipulates that a registered distributor must prioritize client interests, ensuring that the distribution of capital market products is never motivated by the pursuit of extra remuneration.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 24. A registered distributor shall not indulge in any manipulative, fraudulent or deceptive practices. 25
summary: Clause 24 explicitly prohibits a registered distributor from engaging in any manipulative, fraudulent, or deceptive practices.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 25. A registered distributor shall hold valid registration with the IFSCA at all times, if applicable, and shall comply with all applicable laws, code of conduct and norms related to qualifications and experiences of its principal officer and other employees. Advertisement Code for Distributors 26
summary: Clause 25 requires a registered distributor to maintain valid IFSCA registration and comply with all applicable laws, codes of conduct, and qualification/experience norms for its principal officer and employees.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: Advertisement Code for Distributors 26. Advertisements shall be accurate, true, fair, clear, complete, unambiguous and concise. 27
summary: Clause 26 mandates that all advertisements issued by distributors must be accurate, true, fair, clear, complete, unambiguous, and concise.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 27. Advertisements shall not contain statements which are false, misleading, biased or deceptive, based on assumption/projections and shall not contain any testimonials or any ranking based on any criteria. 28
summary: Clause 27 prohibits advertisements from containing false, misleading, biased, or deceptive statements, including those based on assumptions, projections, testimonials, or rankings.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 28. Advertisements shall not be so designed as likely to be misunderstood or likely to disguise the significance of any statement. Advertisements shall not contain statements which directly or by implication or by omission may mislead the client. 29
summary: Clause 28 requires that advertisements be designed to avoid misunderstanding or disguising the significance of statements, prohibiting any content that misleads clients through direct claims, implications, or omissions.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 29. Advertisements shall not carry any slogan that is exaggerated or unwarranted or slogan that is inconsistent with or unrelated to the nature and risk and return profile of the capital market product or service. 30
summary: Section 29 prohibits advertisements from using exaggerated, unwarranted, or misleading slogans that are inconsistent with the risk and return profile of capital market products or services.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 30. Advertisements shall not be so framed as to exploit the lack of experience or knowledge of the clients. Extensive use of technical or legal terminology or complex language and the inclusion of excessive details which may detract the clients should be avoided. 31
summary: Section 30 mandates that advertisements must not exploit client inexperience and should avoid overly complex technical or legal terminology that obscures essential information.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 31. Advertisements shall contain information which is timely and consistent with the disclosures made in the private placement memorandum, disclosure document or the basic document, by whatever name called, which is created by the issuer / service provider and explains the characteristics of the
summary: Section 31 requires that advertisements contain timely information consistent with the disclosures provided in the issuer's private placement memorandum or primary disclosure document.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: provider and explains the characteristics of the capital market product or service
summary: This segment clarifies that advertisements must accurately reflect the specific characteristics of the capital market product or service as defined by the provider.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: G. Investment Advisers 1. An investment adviser shall seek from its clients, information about their financial situation, investment experience and investment objectives relevant to the services to be provided and maintain confidentiality of such information. 2
summary: Investment advisers are required under Section G(1) to collect and maintain the confidentiality of client financial situations, investment experience, and objectives.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 2. An investment adviser shall make adequate disclosures of relevant material information while dealing with its clients. 3. An investment Adviser shall ensure that fees charged to the clients is fair and reasonable. H. Investment Bankers 1
summary: Sections G(2) and G(3) mandate that investment advisers provide adequate material disclosures to clients and ensure that all fees charged are fair and reasonable.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: H. Investment Bankers 1. An investment banker shall ensure that adequate disclosures are made to the investors in a timely manner in accordance with the applicable regulations and guidelines so as to enable them to make a balanced and informed decision. 2
summary: Section H(1) requires investment bankers to provide timely and adequate disclosures to investors to facilitate balanced and informed decision-making.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 2. An investment banker shall endeavour to ensure that the investors are provided with true and adequate information without making any misleading or exaggerated claims or any misrepresentation and are made aware of the attendant risks before taking any investment decision. 3
summary: Section H(2) obligates investment bankers to provide truthful information, avoid misleading or exaggerated claims, and ensure investors are aware of all attendant risks.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 3. An investment banker shall ensure that copies of the prospectus, offer document, letter of offer or any other related document is made available to the investors at the time of issue or the offer. 4
summary: Section H(3) mandates that investment bankers provide investors with copies of the prospectus, offer document, or letter of offer at the time of the issue.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 4. An investment banker shall not discriminate amongst its clients, save and except on ethical and commercial considerations. 5. An investment banker shall maintain arm’s-length relationship between the investment banking activity and any other activity. 6
summary: Sections H(4) and H(5) prohibit investment bankers from discriminating among clients without ethical or commercial justification and require an arm's-length relationship between investment banking and other activities.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 6. An investment banker shall demarcate the responsibilities of the various intermediaries clearly so as to avoid any conflict or confusion in their job description. I. IBU acting as banker to an issue 1
summary: Investment bankers must clearly demarcate responsibilities among intermediaries to prevent conflicts or confusion regarding job descriptions, specifically concerning IBU acting as a banker to an issue.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: I. IBU acting as banker to an issue 1. A banker to an issue shall not allow blank application forms bearing brokers stamp to be kept at the bank premises or peddled anywhere near the entrance of the premises. 2
summary: Bankers to an issue are prohibited from allowing blank application forms with broker stamps to be stored on bank premises or distributed near the entrance.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 2. A banker to an issue shall not accept applications after office hours or after the date of closure of the issue or on bank holidays. 3. A banker to an issue shall not part with the issue proceeds until listing permission is granted by the stock exchange to the body corporate. 4
summary: Bankers to an issue must not accept applications after office hours, post-closure, or on bank holidays, and are prohibited from releasing issue proceeds until the stock exchange grants listing permission.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 4. A banker to an issue shall not delay in issuing the final certificate pertaining to the collection figures to the lead manager and the body corporate. J. Research Entities 1
summary: Bankers to an issue are required to issue final collection figure certificates to the lead manager and the body corporate without delay.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: J. Research Entities 1. A research entity shall act with due skill, care and diligence and shall ensure that the research report is prepared after thorough analysis. 2
summary: Research entities must exercise due skill, care, and diligence, ensuring all research reports are based on thorough analysis.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 2. A research entity shall effectively address conflict of interest which may affect the impartiality of its research analysis and research report and shall make appropriate disclosures to address the same. 3
summary: Research entities must effectively manage conflicts of interest that could compromise research impartiality and provide appropriate disclosures.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 3. A research entity or its employees or research analysts shall not engage in insider trading or front running or front running of its own research report 4. A research entity shall maintain confidentiality of report till the report is made public. 5
summary: Research entities, employees, and analysts are prohibited from insider trading or front-running research reports and must maintain report confidentiality until public release.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II

content: 5. A research entity shall observe high professional standard while preparing research report. 6. The senior management of a research entity shall bear primary responsibility for ensuring the maintenance of appropriate standards of conduct and adherence to proper procedures. K
summary: Research entities must uphold high professional standards, with senior management bearing primary responsibility for ensuring adherence to conduct standards and procedures.

metadata.contained_chapter: Schedule II
